subject: Cumulative and Survival Distribution Functions
question: Suppose that the cumulative distribution function is given by $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 0 \\ \frac{x}{4} & 0\le x < 1 \\ \frac{1}{2} + \frac{x-1}{4} & 1\le x < 2 \\ \frac{11}{12} & 2\le x < 3 \\ 1 & 3\le x \end{cases}$ (a) Find $\Pr(X = i)$, $i = 1, 2, 3$. (b) Find $\Pr(\frac{1}{2} < X < \frac{3}{2})$.
answer: (a) $\Pr(X = 1) = \Pr(X\le 1) - \Pr(X < 1) = F(1) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{4}$. $\Pr(X = 2) = \Pr(X\le 2) - \Pr(X < 2) = F(2) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(2-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \frac{11}{12} - \left(\frac{1}{2} - \frac{2-1}{4}\right) = \frac{1}{6}$. $\Pr(X = 3) = \Pr(X\le 3) - \Pr(X < 3) = F(3) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(3-\frac{1}{n}\right) = 1 - \frac{11}{12} = \frac{1}{12}$ (b) 0.5
explanation: Answer: (a) $\Pr(X = 1) = \Pr(X\le 1) - \Pr(X < 1) = F(1) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{4}$. $\Pr(X = 2) = \Pr(X\le 2) - \Pr(X < 2) = F(2) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(2-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \frac{11}{12} - \left(\frac{1}{2} - \frac{2-1}{4}\right) = \frac{1}{6}$. $\Pr(X = 3) = \Pr(X\le 3) - \Pr(X < 3) = F(3) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(3-\frac{1}{n}\right) = 1 - \frac{11}{12} = \frac{1}{12}$ (b) 0.5
